ddls:
CREATE TABLE "infections" (
  "infection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each infection record */,
  "iss_date" TEXT /* Date of the infection */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the age group */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region */,
  "cases" INTEGER /* Number of cases */,
  "time_period_id" INTEGER /* ID of the time period */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the infected individuals */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the infected individuals */,
  "comorbidities" TEXT /* Comorbidities of the infected individuals */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("infection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_infections_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_infections_time_period_id FOREIGN KEY ("time_period_id") REFERENCES time_periods ("time_period_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_infections_age_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_group_id") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vaccinations" (
  "vaccination_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vaccination record */,
  "iss_date" TEXT /* Date of the vaccination */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the age group */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region */,
  "doses" INTEGER /* Number of doses */,
  "time_period_id" INTEGER /* ID of the time period */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the vaccinated individuals */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the vaccinated individuals */,
  "vaccine_type" TEXT /* Type of vaccine administered */,
  "dose_number" INTEGER /* Dose number of the vaccine */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vaccination_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vaccinations_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vaccinations_time_period_id FOREIGN KEY ("time_period_id") REFERENCES time_periods ("time_period_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vaccinations_age_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_group_id") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_groups" (
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Range of ages in the group (e.g. 0-9, 10-19) */,
  "average_age" REAL /* Average age of the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the region */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the region (in square kilometers) */,
  "density" REAL /* Population density of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_periods" (
  "time_period_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each time period */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the time period */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the time period */,
  "period_type" TEXT /* Type of the time period (e.g., monthly, quarterly, yearly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("time_period_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic record */,
  "age_group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the age group */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the demographic group */,
  "gender_distribution" TEXT /* Distribution of gender within the demographic group */,
  "ethnicity_distribution" TEXT /* Distribution of ethnicity within the demographic group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_age_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_group_id") REFERENCES age_groups ("age_group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "infection_vaccination_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "infection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the infection */,
  "vaccination_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vaccination */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of the relationship (e.g., direct, indirect) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_infection_vaccination_relationships_vaccination_id FOREIGN KEY ("vaccination_id") REFERENCES vaccinations ("vaccination_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_infection_vaccination_relationships_infection_id FOREIGN KEY ("infection_id") REFERENCES infections ("infection_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each infection record

Range of ages in the group (e.g. 0-9, 10-19)
Average age of the age group
